memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to complete a meaningful scholarship application requiring three hours of focused work, or I could choose the fun event for enjoyment.
Starting the application early creates buffer for potential errors, reducing Thursday stress. The fun event remains a flexible option later in the week.
After 2 hours, I've only completed 42% of the required 3 hours. The application remains largely untouched despite significant time investment.
I've made minimal progress on the scholarship application, yet I missed a chance at immediate enjoyment. With high stress and low energy, continuing this slow pace may increase future strain.
I spent one hour on the scholarship application, slightly improving progress from 0.56 to 0.60 despite high stress and low energy.
I spent two more hours on the scholarship application, raising progress to 68%, but still lag behind the three-hour target with low energy and high stress.

Progress increased to 72% in one hour, but energy remains critically low and stress is high. With only three hours total needed, the remaining time may not be enough to complete the task without burning out further.
Progress jumped from 72% to 80% in two hours, nearly meeting the deadline. However, energy is critically low and stress remains high.
After only 2 hours of sleep, my energy is critically low and stress remains high. I must prioritize rest over completing the scholarship application to avoid burnout.
I gained 0.07 progress in 1.5 hours, reaching 87% of the required work. Despite nearing completion, my energy remains critically low and stress is high.
Worked 1.5 hours to reach 93% on the scholarship application, but energy remains critically low and stress is high despite nearing completion.
The scholarship application is now complete, but my energy remains critically low and stress stays high. I must prioritize rest over further work.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
